subject: Mathematics
title: Madhava: Calculus Before Newton
description: A quick, practical guide to Madhava: Calculus Before Newton.

hook.text: Two centuries before Newton, a mathematician in Kerala, India was already using infinite series.

buildup.text: Madhava discovered series expansions for sine, cosine, and pi around the 14th century.

discovery.text: His work anticipated Taylor series—a tool usually credited to 18th-century Europeans.

twist.text: His results were passed down orally and in palm-leaf manuscripts, never reaching Europe.

climax.text: Modern historians now call his school the Kerala School of Astronomy and Mathematics.

punchline.text: Calculus may have been invented in India first.

quiz.question: What did Madhava discover before European mathematicians?
